AI for Operational Blueprint (OB – Knowledge Management): AI for OB is a digital tool that helps staff quickly find clear and consistent information they need to support people in using Services Australia’s payments and services. It is designed to benefit frontline staff and, in turn, the public, by making it easier to locate the right guidance without searching through long or complex documents. The initiative was developed to address delays, confusion, and errors caused by difficult information searches, and to give staff more time to focus on helping people with their individual needs.

CLIKChat – an internal chatbot that supports Department of Veterans’ Affairs that summarises information from publicly available government sources – is designed to help staff easily navigate publicly available policy and compensation information and aims to improve the speed and accuracy of information for claims teams. The tool draws solely on public, non-personal information.

TM Checker is IP Australia's free trade mark availability check designed to lower the bar of entry for small to medium businesses by simplifying the learning process of a complex IP system. The tool allows customers to check the potential registrability of their trademarks and then proceed through the application process. TM Checker guides customers through a step-by-step process. It leverages AI-powered search tools to identify potential issues. This includes similarity and distinctiveness, the most common reasons for rejection.

Artificial Intelligence Virtual Assistant (AIVA): Automates pay intake, calculations, and document handling, with all outputs reviewed, validated, and approved by compensation professionals.

The Compensation Virtual Assistant chatbot provides timely, reliable answers drawn from over 80,000 business rules, 150+ collective agreements, and more than 40,000 pages of directives, terms and conditions, and salary tables.

AIVA is a key component of the Government of Canada’s compensation services modernization. It supports the transformation of the HR-to-Pay intake process from a manual, transaction-based model to an efficient, data-driven, and client-centric approach. By combining intelligent automation with a user-friendly interface, AIVA reduces administrative burden, accelerates intake processing, and enables Compensation Advisors to focus on higher-value work.

Title: Breast Cancer (MASA)
Application Domain Healthcare
	 
Technology Domain Computer vision
Brief:
Early detection is the most powerful tool in the fight against breast cancer. The Breast Cancer (MASA) AI-driven diagnostic solution supports radiologists and oncologists, enhancing the accuracy of mammogram interpretations, ensuring that life-saving diagnoses happen with an accuracy close to 90% for Egyptian women.
Description: 
Breast cancer is the most common cancer occurring in women worldwide. Early diagnosis of breast cancer can lead to proper treatment of affected patients, increase breast cancer survival rates, and significant reduction of treatment costs, personal trauma, and social burden. Screening for early diagnosis and treatment is a major step towards fighting the disease. Using artificial intelligence for screening and early detection can help with the process.
The Breast Cancer (MASA) solution has been developed using artificial intelligence, specifically computer vision techniques, to assist radiologists and oncologists in breast cancer detection during mammogram-based screening. The center in partnership with the Baheya Foundation created a reliable, and high-quality data set that was be used for training the AI models using mammograms from 60K Egyptian women.
The solution analyzes mammogram images to detect abnormal masses, classify them as benign or malignant, identify calcifications, detect asymmetrical features between breasts, while considering the breast tissue density according to the ACR classification. Results are presented according to the global BI-RADS standard. The solution accuracy is over 90% for Egyptian women.
With the deployment of the solution in Baheya hospitals, and solution launch in August 2025, the solution will help automate some of the breast cancer diagnosis tasks, aiding medical staff, reducing the examination costs, increasing the efficiency of this process, and making it available to a larger number of women for periodic checkup.

1818 MIA Chatbot - hybrid chatbot with text-to-speech and speech-to-text functionalities to support clients of various connected public services. The MIA chatbot is an in‑house, custom‑developed hybrid chatbot service that we have been using at the 1818 Government Hotline since 2022 not to replace human intelligence, but to support and enhance operators' work. In 2025, MIA was already able to respond to 63% of customer inquiries without operator intervention. However, in more complex life situations - where precise answers are required and hallucination‑related errors must be avoided - the chatbot redirects the conversation to a human operator. These cases fall into two categories: in most of them, the chatbot still provides answer suggestions to the operator, who can select from them, significantly speeding up response times. In the more complicated cases making up a few percent of all questions, the operator provides the final answer directly.
Currently, MIA does not use a large language model; therefore, it does not perform LLM‑based analysis of customer questions and does not search in any RAG-model knowledge base. Instead, it learns exclusively from conversations between operators and customers, and only provides automated responses for pre‑validated question‑answer pairs.

Agente de Indicaciones (En piloto) - modelo entrenado para contestar preguntas relacionadas con los procesos de Contrataciones Públicas acotados a la normativa actual. 2do Piloto próximo a poner en ejecución - "Preanálisis de criterios técnicos aplicables al flujo de revisión que aplica la Dirección de Procedimiento Excepcional", llamado "PREDIPEC", que implica la identificación automática de presunción de división de materia, análisis de contenido, e identificación de patrones sobre los documentos que han sido proporcionados durante este proceso de contrataciones públicas.  Esto permitirá un preanalisis centrado en los criterios técnicos que hoy día llevan a cabo los abogados de la Dirección de Procedimiento Excepcional (DIPEC) pudiéndose agilizar la carga de trabajo de análisis manual que hoy día se realiza en este área.

AMALIA - AMALIA is the first LLM designed for European Portuguese, with the aim of ensuring Portugal's linguistic and cultural sovereignty. The base version was launched in SEP25 and the full multimodal version (capable of interpreting text, images and video) arrives mid-2026. Use cases will focus on the areas of education, culture, speech, media, science and public administration. The model will be openly available so that it can be used and improved by scientists and development teams on any platform

It  benefits citizens, foreign residents and businesses by making information easier to find and understand. It was developed to cut bureaucratic friction (complex navigation, language barriers and long waits) while reducing workload on public administration

LIA - LIA is a chat-based search engine specialized in the legislation published in Portugal Official Gazette. It tries to avoid the challenges most citizens (non-legal trained) have when searching legislation. Lia uses a hybrid architecture to communicate with the users and to return search results. The first version of Lia was able to respond based only on current consolidated legislation. The second version will be able to respond on current consolidated legislation and non-consolidated fragmented legislation. MODE – The Waste Monitoring Model: Detects waste and fraud in healthcare expenditures. Developed with a hybrid approach combining business rules and unsupervised decision-tree learning, it analyzes billing and prescription data to identify anomalies, assigning risk scores from 0 to 1 to patients, doctors, pharmacies, and providers, with higher scores indicating greater risk.

Detect SNS 24: Early detection and prevention of skin cancer. Users can capture images of skin lesions using their smartphones, which are then automatically analyzed by an AI model that compares the patterns against validated clinical image databases. The system assigns a risk score to each lesion, enabling early identification of suspicious cases, reducing the time to medical evaluation, and providing objective support to healthcare professionals in clinical decision-making.

Predictive Coding: Translates data recorded in Electronic Health Records into standardized numeric or alphanumeric formats, allowing diagnoses and procedures to be structured and normalized. High-quality coding is essential not only for accurate hospital financing but also for medical research, epidemiological surveillance, and health system planning.

Tele-Stroke Platform – Ministry of Health: The Tele-Stroke Platform is a virtual urgent-care service provided through Seha Virtual Hospital to support stroke management across Saudi Arabia. The Tele-Stroke Platform provides urgent and specialized care for stroke patients by connecting peripheral hospitals with advanced stroke centers through a virtual medical platform. The platform uses artificial intelligence to support real-time CT scan analysis, accelerate diagnosis, reduce unnecessary patient transfers, improve the utilization of specialized medical personnel, lower operational costs, and enhance the quality of healthcare services.

Rased AI Product – Zakat, Tax and Customs Authority:
Rased is an artificial intelligence solution launched by the Zakat, Tax and Customs Authority to forecast incoming and outgoing vehicle movements at land ports. It uses advanced algorithms and predictive analytics based on internal and external factors affecting vehicle flow, providing forecasts at the daily and hourly levels. The solution supports proactive border readiness, improves operational planning and resource allocation, reduces congestion, enhances security, and improves the experience of travelers and land freight operators.

Turkish Brain Project: By analyzing MRI scans of patients with brain tumors, artificial intelligence algorithms can be trained, and with the help of advanced scientific techniques, abnormal areas in the brain can be identified and subsequently classified.

Digital Eye – Mammography Project: aims to revolutionize the early diagnosis of breast cancer. The goal is to enhance the accuracy of radiologists’ mammography evaluation processes and minimize issues arising from human error through a newly developed AI-powered decision support system. The AI model analyzes mammography images to identify high-risk cases and prioritizes these for review by radiologists.

Digital Eye – Liver Project: The project performs AI-powered mass detection and classification. The AI models developed were integrated into the reporting system of the Ministry of Health’s Cancer Early Diagnosis Screening and Education Center, and the system’s first version was put into use. This provided support to relevant specialists for the faster and prioritized reporting of patients with potentially malignant masses. Additionally, an AI-based decision support system was provided to radiologists for testing purposes during the review of patients’ mammography images at hospitals affiliated with Başkent and Ankara Universities.

Its objectives are to reduce bureaucracy, save time and labor, lower error rates, and provide efficient and transparent human resources management.

Intergrated Financial Management System (Oracle Enterprise Business Suite) Configuration Support – an NLP agent extracts a snapshot of the current system configuration state, covering workflow and business logic, security rules, role segregation, and cross-MDA data access conditions. It then analyses existing configurations to recommend workflow optimisations, security hardening measures, and data validation checks aligned with best practices. Rule-based integrity checks validate that proposed configurations will not violate data integrity, security, or compliance requirements. AI-assisted test scripts and data scenarios are generated to validate new configurations in the test environment before migration to production.
